# Backtest Report: Week of 2025-07-11 to 2026-07-10

## Assumptions
- Data: yfinance 1-min RTH bars; walk-forward replay through SignalRunner.detect_signals
- $1000 risk per trade, 2R target -> win +$2000, loss -$1000, scratch = R x $1000 at EOD close
- Stop+target same bar counted as loss (conservative)
- Repeat fires of same setup within 30 min deduped

## Summary
- Traded signals (A+/A/B, viable stop): **1811** | 600W 1207L 4 scratch | win rate 33.2% (of decided)
- Simulated P&L (traded all A+/A/B): **$-11352.39**
- C-grade alerts (alert-only per SPEC2): 1019 | D filtered: 14730 | tight-stop skips: 683

## Findings & Recommendations
- A+/A win rate 32% vs B/C 33% -> grading NOT predictive this week - review PA grade criteria
- D-grade filter: filtered signals would have won 32% -> filter justified (<50%)
- 84% rule (Lesson 6 canonical 2026-07-06: solid B&R stop-out arms one re-entry on the reclaim close, ORIGINAL stop + target): 255 triggers, fired win rate 31%.
- 84% live wiring: armed per-symbol off paper stop-outs in live_scanner (2026-07-05). Requires --paper mode; signal-only runs have no stop-out feedback.
- Best setup: one_candle_rule (38%) | worst: reentry_84_rule (31%)
- C-grade alerts (1019, alert-only per SPEC2) would have won 32% - similar to traded grades; alert-only demotion costs little.
